resume.pageDescription: Sing Chan's resume - 25+ years experience in UX, product management, and software development.
resume.title: Résumé

header.tagline: Technical Product Leader & User Experience Architect – Bridging Usability, Engineering & Business Strategy

professionalSummary.heading: Professional Summary
professionalSummary.content: Technical product and engineering leader with 25+ years of experience spanning enterprise SaaS, front-end architecture, and cross-functional product delivery. Built teams, UX practices, and engineering standards from the ground up at multiple organizations. Mentored developers from co-op through senior roles while staying hands-on in React, TypeScript, and .NET/C#. Led 0-to-1 product development in FedRAMP-certified and SOC-2 compliant environments, from customer discovery and interaction design through cloud infrastructure optimization. Daily AI-first development practitioner. Extensive client portfolio including government, Fortune 500 companies, and organizations in highly regulated industries.

roles.title: VP, Product
contributions: Eliminated over $360,000 CAD in annualized hosting costs by leading an Azure Cloud infrastructure audit that identified and implemented 40% reductions
Drove product execution for enterprise solutions, leading cross-functional UX, QA, engineering, and CloudOps teams
Led QA automation framework development, improving test coverage and reducing regression defects
Partnered with CloudOps to monitor system health, maintain SLA compliance, and ensure platform reliability
Handled Tier 3 support escalations and built internal troubleshooting and data fix-up tools for the support team
Designed and delivered product knowledge onboarding program for new team members, reducing time-to-productivity by over 50%, from one month to two weeks

Planned, developed, tested, and shipped Collabspace Export Downloader solo in under a month using an AI-first SDLC with Claude Code. .NET 9 / MAUI 9 WCAG 2.2 AA-compliant desktop application with 4-language localization

roles.title: Product Manager
contributions: Conducted customer discovery interviews and stakeholder sessions to define feature priorities and validate product-market fit
Translated requirements into actionable product roadmap priorities, aligning business needs with engineering capacity
Managed product backlog and sprint planning in collaboration with engineering teams, improving delivery predictability

roles.title: User Experience Architect
contributions: Architected the front-end platform evolution from Collabware CLM to Collabspace, transitioning from on-premises SharePoint to cloud-native SaaS
Established the component design system, coding standards, and UX development practices adopted by the engineering team

roles.title: User Experience Developer
contributions: Hired as the first UX developer in the organization to bridge communication gaps between design and engineering teams
Built the UX development practice from the ground up, introduced front-end process patterns and standards, and evangelized the UX developer role
Mentored new UX developers and grew the front-end team to 8 developers
Took on group management and resource allocation responsibilities

roles.title: Interactive Producer
contributions: Managed resources, project schedules, and vendor and customer relationships

roles.title: Interactive Developer
contributions: Developed grASP, a modular and extensible in-house CMS used on Grey interactive projects
Developed and maintained Grey's external and internal web sites and applications

roles.title: Lead Developer (Contract)
contributions: Managed and led development of interactive projects, from the database layer all the way up to the front-end

roles.title: Flash and User Experience Developer (Contract)
contributions: Developed Flash applications, client-side scripting and XHTML/CSS markup for LLDT projects
Provided interface design and programming assistance with development of CustomBlox, LLDT's ASP.Net CMS solution

entries.program: Undergraduate studies in Computer Science and Economics
